title: One Voice, Many Tongues: Cross-Lingual Voice Cloning for Scientific Speech
abstract: arXiv:2604.26136v2 Announce Type: replace-cross  Abstract: Preserving a speaker's voice identity while generating speech in a different language remains a fundamental challenge in spoken language technology, particularly in specialized domains such as scientific communication. In this paper, we address this challenge through our system submission to the International Conference on Spoken Language Translation (IWSLT 2026), the Cross-Lingual Voice Cloning shared task. First, we evaluate several state-of-the-art voice cloning models for cross-lingual speech generation of scientific texts in Arabic, Chinese, and French. Then, we build voice cloning systems based on the OmniVoice foundation model. We employ data augmentation via multi-model ensemble distillation from the ACL 60/60 corpus. We investigate the effect of using this synthetic data for fine-tuning, demonstrating improvements in intelligibility (WER & CER) and speaker similarity (SIM), with gains varying across languages.

Title: One Voice, Many Tongues: Cross-Lingual Voice Cloning for Scientific Speech
Abstract: arXiv:2604.26136v2 Announce Type: replace-cross  Abstract: Preserving a speaker's voice identity while generating speech in a different language remains a fundamental challenge in spoken language technology, particularly in specialized domains such as scientific communication. In this paper, we address this challenge through our system submission to the International Conference on Spoken Language Translation (IWSLT 2026), the Cross-Lingual Voice Cloning shared task. First, we evaluate several state-of-the-art voice cloning models for cross-lingual speech generation of scientific texts in Arabic, Chinese, and French. Then, we build voice cloning systems based on the OmniVoice foundation model. We employ data augmentation via multi-model ensemble distillation from the ACL 60/60 corpus. We investigate the effect of using this synthetic data for fine-tuning, demonstrating improvements in intelligibility (WER & CER) and speaker similarity (SIM), with gains varying across languages.
translated_title: 一种声音，多种语言：面向科学演讲的跨语言语音克隆
translated_abstract: 在生成不同语言的语音时保留说话者的声音身份，仍然是口语技术中的一个基本挑战，尤其是在科学交流等专业领域。本文通过我们提交至国际口语翻译会议（IWSLT 2026）跨语言语音克隆共享任务的系统来应对这一挑战。首先，我们评估了几种最先进的语音克隆模型，用于阿拉伯语、中文和法语科学文本的跨语言语音生成。然后，我们基于OmniVoice基础模型构建了语音克隆系统。我们通过ACL 60/60语料库的多模型集成蒸馏进行了数据增强。我们研究了使用这些合成数据进行微调的效果，证明了在可懂度（WER和CER）和说话者相似度（SIM）方面的提升，且不同语言的增益有所不同。
tldr: 本文通过评估多种语音克隆模型并基于OmniVoice模型结合数据增强微调，在科学演讲的跨语言语音克隆中显著提升了可懂度和说话者相似度。
en_tdlr: This paper improves intelligibility and speaker similarity in cross-lingual voice cloning for scientific speech by evaluating multiple models and fine-tuning the OmniVoice model with data augmentation via multi-model ensemble distillation.
